Fix Beds24 V1 error code handling: use String() comparison, add code 2002

- 1002: API key not found (invalid key)

The simulations hardcoded ~/.claude/skills/ as the install path even

location that gets symlinked to tool-specific paths by the installer.

commit.message:
fix: handle autobiography_draft as string from DB

Problem: Supabase returns autobiography_draft as JSON string, not parsed object.
This caused chapter1Draft to be undefined, hiding the draft section.

Solution: Parse string to JSON if needed in ArchiveView.
Handle both string (from DB) and object (already parsed) cases.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix Railway sync service: bulk inserts, PgBouncer compat, date types

- Fix urlencode in Mavely auth (httpx.QueryParams has no .encode())
- Pass datetime.date objects for DATE columns (asyncpg rejects strings)
- Add statement_cache_size=0 for Supabase PgBouncer transaction mode
- Reuse single DB connection per sync run (was creating new conn per execute)
- Make HTTP trigger endpoints fire-and-forget (return 202 immediately)
- Switch to executemany for batch inserts (277 links in one round-trip)
- Reduce transaction window 90d→30d with 50-page safety limit
- Skip mavely_transactions insert (table has legacy 0002 schema; dashboard uses platform_earnings only)

Both Mavely sync (277 links, ~2s) and LTK sync (7d+30d) now completing successfully.

commit.message:
Add sideways regime routing: logreg for SP500, mlp for R2K

In sideways markets the 4-model ensemble mean never reaches 70% WR
because catboost/v22 cap below 70% and drag the average down. Only
logreg_buy (SP500, prob >= 0.80) and mlp_buy (R2K, prob >= 0.85)
have reliable monotone WR lookups in sideways.

Bear/bull routing unchanged — still uses 4-model mean WR.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix walking time calculation — use distance-based estimate instead of OSRM duration

OSRM demo server returns driving-speed durations even for foot profile.
Now walking time is calculated from route distance at 80 m/min (~3 mph).
Driving time still uses OSRM duration. Route geometry always uses driving
profile for reliability. Bump SW cache to v7.

commit.message:
fix(theme): prevent FOUC for light-mode OS users

- Add inline script in <head> of index.html and 404.html that reads
  localStorage and prefers-color-scheme, then synchronously sets
  data-theme="light" and colorScheme="light" before CSS renders
- Eliminates dark background flash (~200ms) seen by users whose OS
  prefers light mode on first/cold page load
- CSP updated in both files: script-src adds SHA-256 hash of the
  exact inline script content so it is allowed without 'unsafe-inline'

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Photo editor: fire-and-forget + poll for edits, fix empty response handling

Photo editor was failing because n8n takes 1-3+ minutes for AI photo
editing, causing the Netlify function to timeout and return an empty
body. response.json() then threw "Unexpected end of JSON input".

Fix - PhotoEditor._generate():
- Fire-and-forget POST using ?fireAndForget=true query param
- Show persistent loading state: "Editing your photo — this may
  take a minute..."
- Poll content_deliveries every 15s checking if the specific post's
  photo_url changed from the original
- When edit detected: update preview image, show success message,
  update iteration counter
- Timeout after 3 minutes with amber warning message
- Include delivery_id, platform, post_index in payload so n8n
  knows which row to update
- Stop polling on close, reset, or new generate

Fix - proxy-webhook.js:
- Add fireAndForget=true mode: immediately returns 202 Accepted,
  sends request to n8n in background
- Handle empty upstream response body gracefully instead of
  failing on JSON.parse
- Wrap non-JSON text responses in { raw: text } object
- Add logging for upstream status and body length

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Add AVD BYOL eligibility rules and regulated-industry compliance floors to system prompt

BYOL eligibility: explicitly list which SKUs qualify (Business Premium, E3, E5, F3,
Windows Enterprise E3/E5) and which do not (Basic, Standard, Apps for Business).
Fixes incorrect recommendations that claimed Business Basic qualifies for AVD BYOL.

F3 scope guard: F3 is BYOL-eligible but restricted to frontline/kiosk workers only;
prevent recommendation for knowledge workers needing full Office and personal mailboxes.

Regulated-industry floor: healthcare/HIPAA, legal, and financial orgs get E3 minimum
due to 1-year audit log retention, Litigation Hold, and eDiscovery Standard requirements.
Business Premium (90-day audit logs) is explicitly excluded for regulated workloads.

No Windows Server RDS CAL required for AVD when user holds eligible per-user M365 license.

commit.message:
feat(api): add correlation IDs, global exception handling, and security event logging (MGG-101) (#69)

* feat(api): add correlation IDs, global exception handling, and security event logging (MGG-101)

Replace per-controller try/catch blocks with centralized middleware for
consistent error handling, request tracing, and security observability.

- Add CorrelationIdMiddleware (reads/generates X-Correlation-ID, enriches Serilog LogContext)
- Add GlobalExceptionHandlerMiddleware (ProblemDetails for all errors, DuplicateEntity→409, KeyNotFound→404)
- Add Serilog request logging with host/user-agent/userId enrichment
- Add JWT OnAuthenticationFailed/OnChallenge security event handlers
- Add AuthorizationLoggingHandler for denied authorization logging
- Update MustResetPasswordMiddleware to return ProblemDetails
- Remove dead UseExceptionHandler("/Error") and UseHsts() from non-dev path
- Add CorrelationId to console output template with Microsoft log level overrides
- Clean up 18 controllers: remove 96 catch blocks, ~115 LogDebug statements, dead constants
- Remove ILogger from 4 controllers that no longer log
- Update 10 controller test files to verify exception propagation
- Add 14 new middleware tests

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(api): restore HSTS for non-development environments (MGG-101)

UseHsts() was inadvertently removed along with the dead UseExceptionHandler("/Error") call.

commit.message:
fix: Use local node path for hooks instead of npx to avoid registry lookups (v0.15.31)

Hook commands now use `node <path>/claude-recall-cli.js hook run ...`
instead of `npx claude-recall@latest hook run ...`. This eliminates
npm registry lookups on every tool call and session end, fixing
ETARGET errors when the registry hasn't propagated a new version yet.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Support structured output with tools for both provider types

Messages API: serialize output_schema as output_config.format (native
json_schema mode, now GA). Chat Completions API: transparent two-step
call in the runner — first request includes tools (no schema), second
request applies schema (no tools) when the model completes without tool
calls. Usage from both calls is summed.

Remove validate_params rejection of tools + output_schema in
chat_completions provider. Fix pre-existing unused import in
credential.rs.

commit.message:
fix: SEO timeout + smart website context extraction

- Increase SEO/geo mode timeout from 20s → 90s (o4-mini reasoning takes longer)
- Add OPENAI_SEO_TIMEOUT_MS env var for overriding
- Replace naive text.slice(0,3000) with structured profile extraction:
  - Normalizes setext headings (=== / ---) before parsing
  - Captures name/credential/location from top of page (up to 400 chars)
  - Captures wanted sections: About Me, Challenges treated, Client focus,
    Methods, Modalities, Services, Approach, Education, Languages, Experience
  - Skips noise sections: Choose a time, Schedule, Billing, Insurance
    Carriers, Reviews — saving tokens for what matters
  - Max 3500 chars total; extracted context is dense and relevant

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(youtube): add site guide, recipe script, and API-based transcript extraction

- Add youtube.mjs recipe script with functions: detectLogin, search,
  extractSearchResults, extractHomeFeed, extractVideoInfo, extractComments,
  extractRelatedVideos, getCaptionTracks, extractTranscript, extractTranscriptText,
  toggleCaptions, playerControl, openVideo, openChannel
- Transcript extraction uses timedtext API with POT (Proof of Origin Token)
  captured from network log, outputs SRT format or plain text (--text flag)
- POT is per-video: reuses any captured URL and swaps lang param for cross-language
- Fix comment time selector: .published-time-text a → a[href*="lc="]
- Add youtube.com.md site guide with selector reference tables
- Update site-guide SKILL.md with recipe script workflow and conventions
- Update CONTRIBUTING.md with async eval example

commit.message:
Sticky name + actions columns with horizontal scroll in roster view

- .roster-grid: overflow-x clip→auto (scrollbar when columns overflow),
  overflow-y: clip (prevents auto from creating vertical scroll container
  so .roster-header sticky-top continues to reference the page viewport)
- .roster-header > div:first-child: position sticky left:0 z-index:9
  background:#eceff1 — name header sticks to left edge while scrolling
- .roster-header > div:last-child: position sticky right:0 z-index:9
  background:#eceff1 — actions header sticks to right edge
- .roster-name: position sticky left:0 z-index:2 background:inherit —
  rider/coach name cell always visible on the left; inherit picks up the
  correct row background for all coach-level and archived variants
- .roster-actions: position sticky right:0 z-index:2 background:inherit —
  View/Edit Full Record button always visible on the right
- @media (max-width:768px): position:static on both roster-name and
  roster-actions — disables desktop sticky in the mobile flex layout

commit.message:
fix(claude-irc): security hardening, session resilience, and topic management

- Prevent --name flag impersonation (validate against session marker)
- Block path traversal in peer names (alphanumeric/hyphen/underscore only)
- Fix inbox index mismatch between list and detail view
- Fix board -1 flag parsing error (SetInterspersed)
- Optimize DetectSession: scan markers first, add 500ms timeout to ps calls
- Allow re-registration when daemon is unresponsive (fixes quit+rejoin)
- Clean up orphan inbox/topics directories on `who`
- Add topic --delete and --clear commands
- Deduplicate topic titles (same title overwrites existing)
